revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24618454543|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Araho-jinja]][[en:Araho Shrine]][[ja:荒穂神社]] {{Expand Japanese|荒穂神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Araho Shrine | native_name = 荒穂神社 | image = Araho-jinja 03.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Araho Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.434017|130.509468}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|de|Ninigi|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|eo|Ninigi|es|Ninigi no Mikoto|fa|نینیگی-نو-میکوتو|fr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|id|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|it|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギ|ko|니니기|mni|ꯅꯤꯅꯤꯒꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Ninigi|no|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|pl|Ninigi|pt|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ru|Ниниги но Микото|simple|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|sv|Ninigi|th|นินิงิ โนะ มิโกโตะ|tl|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|tr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|uk|Нініґі но мікото|vi|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|zh|瓊瓊杵尊|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Araho Shrine'''|荒穂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hizen Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * Province|ja|肥前国の式内社一覧|simple|List of Shikinaisha in Hizen Province|qid=Q11611527|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hizen Province}} ** Series ordinal: 3 ** P155: {{ill|Q11490808|ltq=Q11490808|qid=Q11490808|1=Shijiki Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11613317|ltq=Q11613317|qid=Q11613317|1=Yodohime Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Kiyama, Saga|lt=Kiyama|arz|كياما|ceb|Kiyama Chō|en|Kiyama, Saga|envoyage|Kiyama|fa|کیاما، ساگا|fr|Kiyama|id|Kiyama, Saga|it|Kiyama|ja|基山町|ko|기야마정|ms|Kiyama, Saga|pl|Kiyama|ru|Кияма|sv|Kiyama|tl|Kiyama, Saga|tr|Kiyama|uk|Кіяма|vi|Kiyama, Saga|zh|基山町|zhvoyage|基山|qid=Q1206602|1=Kiyama, Saga}} * {{ill|Q906926|ltq=Q906926|qid=Q906926|1=Hizen Province}} * {{ill|Kii district|lt=Kii district|ja|基肄郡|zh|基肄郡|qid=Q10930388|1=Kii district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|33.434017|130.509468}} == image (P18) == * Araho-jinja 03.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|de|Ninigi|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|eo|Ninigi|es|Ninigi no Mikoto|fa|نینیگی-نو-میکوتو|fr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|id|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|it|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギ|ko|니니기|mni|ꯅꯤꯅꯤꯒꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Ninigi|no|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|pl|Ninigi|pt|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ru|Ниниги но Микото|simple|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|sv|Ninigi|th|นินิงิ โนะ มิโกโตะ|tl|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|tr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|uk|Нініґі но мікото|vi|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|zh|瓊瓊杵尊|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} == OpenStreetMap way ID (P10689) == * 341380629 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183318 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11123280|ltq=Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あらほじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4300005004689 == Commons category (P373) == * Araho-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '佐賀県三養基郡基山町宮浦字宮脇2050', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Araho-jinja}} [[EN:Araho Shrine]] [[JA:荒穂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q67989816}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|A shrine in Kiyama Town, Saga Prefecture|A shrine of the same name in Tara Town, Saga Prefecture|Araho Shrine (Tara Town)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Araho Shrine |Image = [[File:Araho-jinja 03.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = Miyawaki 2050, Miyaura, {{ill|lt=Kiyama Town|Q1206602|qid=Q1206602|1=Kiyama, Saga}}, {{ill|lt=Miyaki District|Q1043864|qid=Q1043864|1=Miyaki District, Saga}}, {{ill|lt=Saga Prefecture|Q160420|qid=Q160420|1=Saga Prefecture}} |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|26|01.9|N|130|30|34.4|E|region:JP-41_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017}} |Rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinai-shōsha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|qid=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=Hiwada-buki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Other names = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = September 23 |Rites = Sai Festival (May 1–7) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saga|label=Araho Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} **Araho Shrine** (あらほじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kiyama Town|Q1206602|qid=Q1206602|1=Kiyama, Saga}}, {{ill|lt=Miyaki District|Q1043864|qid=Q1043864|1=Miyaki District, Saga}}, {{ill|lt=Saga Prefecture|Q160420|qid=Q160420|1=Saga Prefecture}}. It is enshrined in the Miyawaki area of Miyaura on the southern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mount Kiyama|Q61679989|qid=Q61679989|1=Mount Kizan}}.[https://www.town.kiyama.lg.jp/kiji003915/3_915_5_7722.pdf The Goshinkō Festival of Araho Shrine] Kōhō Kiyama (Sept. 15, Heisei 29), Kiyama Town Its shrine rank is that of a {{ill|lt=Shikinai shrine|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=minor shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the Engishiki, and it later held the status of a {{ill|lt=Prefectural Shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017}} along with deities of six other subsidiary shrines.[https://www.town.kiyama.lg.jp/kiji0031991/index.html Araho Shrine] (November 28, 2018), Kiyama Town There have been various theories since ancient times regarding the identity of the enshrined deities, but in folklore studies it is considered that the original deity was Araho Tenjin, a natural and musubi (creative life-force) deity, with the Tamadama Stone—a massive granite boulder at the summit of Mount Kii (Mount Kiyama)—serving as an iwakura (sacred rock). According to the *Araho Shrine Engi* from the first year of Jōkyō (1684), the six shrines are listed as follows: at the center, Araho Daimyōjin; to the left, Shimogamo Ōkami; left second, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|qid=Q261637}}; to the right, Hōman Ōkami; right second, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|qid=Q11088805|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyōjin|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}; and right third, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}}. (After this, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} was added.) === History === According to the *Araho Shrine Engi*, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|qid=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} (mid-7th century) by Kanemura no Omi, a descendant of Mononobe no Kanetsura, the provincial governor of Matsura (Kii). The *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}* records that in the second year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|qid=Q24714|1=860}}), the shrine was promoted in rank from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.Entry dated February 8. It is also one of the four shrines of {{ill|lt=Hizen Province|Q906926|qid=Q906926|1=Hizen Province}} listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* (the list of Shikinai shrines of Hizen Province). The shrine buildings were originally located on the summit of Mount Kiyama, but due to warfare during the Sengoku period, they were moved several times before being rebuilt at the present location in the second year of Jōkyō ({{ill|lt=1685|Q7660|qid=Q7660|1=1685}}). The shrine buildings are constructed in the nagare-zukuri style. The worship hall dates from the second year of Bunsei ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|qid=Q6967|1=1819}}), while both the main hall and inner hall were reconstructed in the fifth year of Ansei (1858). In the sixth year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), the shrine was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in the fifteenth year of the {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} era ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|qid=Q18633|1=1940}}), it was elevated to the rank of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals and rites === * New Year’s Day Festival – January 1 * Spring Festival – February 19 * Sai Festival – May 1 for seven days * Summer Festival – July 19 * Chinowa purification rite – from July 31 for three days * Goshinkō Festival – September 23 * Monthly Festival – 19th of every month Araho-jinja 01.jpg|First torii gate Araho-jinja 02.jpg|Second torii gate Araho-jinja.JPG|Shukubō hall and worship hall Camphor tree of Araho Shrine, Kiyama.jpg|Camphor tree === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8FCCfWHS6co&feature=youtu.be Saga Festival Chronicle: The Goshinkō Festival of Araho Shrine (Kiyama Town)] – Saga Television {{Shinto-footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Araho Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Araho Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:基山町の建築物]] [[Category:基山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]]
